step_by_step_thinking: Bolton’s analysis is used to assess the proportionate relationship among all arches, including both dental arches (maxilla and mandible), as well as the interarch relationships. It provides an overall evaluation of tooth-size discrepancy which can help determine any disproportions or discrepancies in sizes between these two major sets of dentition.
raw_response:
Bolton’s analysis is used to assess the proportionate relationship among all arches, including both dental arches (maxilla and mandible), as well as the interarch relationships. It provides an overall evaluation of tooth-size discrepancy which can help determine any disproportions or discrepancies in sizes between these two major sets of dentition.  

Answer: B The correct option for analyzing the disproportion in the size between maxillary and mandibular teeth would be **Bolton's analysis** because it comprehensively evaluates the proportional relationship across multiple points on each jaw, allowing for accurate identification of any significant differences in tooth dimensions.
